records.ety: [L. impenetrabilis; pref. im- not + penetrabilis penetrable: cf. F. impntrable.]

defs: Incapable of being penetrated or pierced; not admitting the passage of other bodies; not to be entered; impervious; as, an impenetrable shield.
quotes: Highest woods impenetrable To star or sunlight.

defs: Having the property of preventing any other substance from occupying the same space at the same time.
Inaccessible, as to knowledge, reason, sympathy, etc.; unimpressible; not to be moved by arguments or motives; as, an impenetrable mind, or heart.
quotes: They will be credulous in all affairs of life, but impenetrable by a sermon of the gospel.
